title: Who were the Manchus?
subtitle: A short N4 reading about the people who founded China's last dynasty.
blocks: The Manchus were a people from the northeast of Asia.

blocks.tr: Long ago, they lived in the north of China.
blocks.g: 〜ていました is the past continuous — an ongoing state in the past ('were living').

jp: せんろっぴゃくよんじゅうよねん
blocks.tr: In 1644 they unified the country and founded a state called the Qing.
blocks.g: 〜という names something: 「清という国」= 'a country called the Qing.' まとめて is the て-form of the kana verb まとめる, linking the two actions.

blocks: The Qing became the last dynasty of imperial China.
Their language and customs were different from those of the Han majority.

blocks.tr: Both their language and their culture were very different.
blocks.g: 〜も〜も means 'both … and …'. ちがいました is the past of the kana verb ちがう ('to differ').
